questions: What is the exact shape of the server's ticket-detail response for relationship fields, and does it match what the CLI needs to display?
Does the server list endpoint return relationship data per item (afterTicket, implementFromTicketId, referencedTicketIds), or only afterTicketId?
How should the CLI resolve multiple ticket references for the --reference flag (comma-separated vs. multiple calls)?
What is the server's error response shape for relationship validation failures, and how does hxFetch surface them to the user?
Can the existing resolveTicket() utility handle the performance implications of resolving multiple references (up to 5 for --reference)?

answers.answer: The server detail endpoint (getTicketDetailForOrganization) returns full relationship data: afterTicketId (string|null), afterTicket (nested: {id, title, status, shortId, mode, approvalStatus}|null), implementFromTicketId (string|null), implementFromTicket (nested: same shape|null), referencedTicketIds (string[]), and referencedTickets (array of nested: {id, title, status, shortId, mode, approvalStatus}). This is sufficient for the CLI's get display.
evidence: helix-global-server/src/services/ticket-service.ts lines 1789-1828: getTicketDetailForOrganization return object includes afterTicketId, afterTicket, implementFromTicketId, implementFromTicket, referencedTicketIds, referencedTickets

answers.answer: The list endpoint returns MORE than diagnosis indicated. Each list item includes: afterTicketId (string|null), afterTicket (nested: {id, title, status, shortId, approvalStatus}|null), implementFromTicketId (string|null), and referencedTicketIds (string[]). This means the CLI list view can show rich dependency info without extra API calls.
evidence: helix-global-server/src/services/ticket-service.ts lines 1674-1688: list response per item includes afterTicketId, afterTicket (nested), implementFromTicketId, referencedTicketIds
helix-global-server/src/services/ticket-service.ts lines 1603-1617: Prisma query includes afterTicket relation in findMany

answers.answer: The CLI should accept comma-separated ticket references for --reference (e.g. --reference RSH-490,RSH-491). Each reference is resolved individually via resolveTicket() to get the internal ID. The server enforces max 5 via Zod schema (z.array().max(5)). Comma-separated avoids shell quoting issues and matches the --repos flag pattern already established in the CLI.
evidence: helix-cli/src/tickets/create.ts lines 61-62: --repos uses comma-separated with split/trim/filter pattern

answers.answer: Server validation errors are returned as JSON { error: 'message' } with appropriate HTTP status codes (400 for most validation failures). hxFetch's buildErrorMessage extracts response text and formats it as 'HTTP 400 Bad Request - message'. The raw error messages from the server are human-readable (e.g. 'Circular dependency detected.', 'implementFromTicketId must reference a RESEARCH mode ticket.'). The CLI should catch these and strip the 'HTTP 400 Bad Request - ' prefix to present clean error text.
evidence: helix-global-server/src/app.ts lines 93-97: HttpError handler returns res.status(error.statusCode).json({ error: error.message })
helix-global-server/src/services/ticket-service.ts lines 668-685, 689-736, 760-777: Specific validation error messages for each relationship type
helix-cli/src/lib/http.ts lines 28-35: buildErrorMessage extracts response text

answers.answer: resolveTicket() fetches the entire ticket list (active, then archived if needed) to match a reference. For --reference with up to 5 values, calling resolveTicket() sequentially would make 2-10 API calls. However, this is acceptable: (a) ticket creation is not a hot path, (b) the alternative (a batch-resolve helper) would be over-engineering for a max of 5 references, and (c) the existing ticket list is cached per call, so optimization could be added later if needed. A pragmatic improvement would be to fetch the list once and call matchTicket() for each reference, but this can be deferred.
evidence: helix-cli/src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts lines 86-167: resolveTicket() fetches active list, then archived if no match

statement: The technical direction is a single-repo change in helix-cli: add --after, --reference, and --implement-from flags to the create command using existing resolveTicket() for reference resolution and getFlag() for parsing; extend TicketDetail and TicketItem types to include relationship fields; add display sections to get and list outputs; and update all documentation surfaces. The server API is fully ready and returns rich relationship data on both detail and list endpoints. Error responses are well-structured JSON with human-readable messages. No new dependencies or abstractions are needed.
